expertise_area: Web and Graphic Design, Agency Leadership

key_insights.insight: The dark side of being a CEO involves a constant stream of cortisol and the slow learning of how to manage it, sometimes leading to panic attacks.
key_insights.quote: the dark side is that there's a long drawn out um kind of pumping out rejection of like cortisol let's just constantly running through your body and you slowly learn to manage it and you have moments certainly at the start of the business where you're having panic attacks

key_insights.insight: Entrepreneurs develop a learned resilience that enables them to handle unexpected bad news with composure and a focus on solutions.
key_insights.quote: That's the resilience, right? That's the resilience where you think, I've got this.

key_insights.insight: Entrepreneurs can develop an addiction to solving tough problems and the relaxation that follows.
key_insights.quote: i think i'm a little bit addicted to the feet almost i feel i feel likeit's a developed addiction where the days that i feel most fulfilled are the days that um at theend of the day at the end of a tough day when i've when things have been resolved and i've gone homeand got into bed and i'm just relaxing

key_insights.insight: Hindsight can be a powerful tool, but it is important to look forward and not dwell on the hard times.
key_insights.quote: we get through those tough times with a certain self-belief resilience and looking forward not dwelling

key_insights.insight: Transparency with employees should have limits; sharing granular financial details can be detrimental.
key_insights.quote: there's a layer there that you shouldn't go past and I've dropped that a littlebit I suppose with my team now you know I don't discuss too much of what's discussed with theaccountants with the senior team

key_insights.insight: The responsibility felt as a CEO increases as headcount grows, making careful financial management even more critical.
key_insights.quote: now that we have a greater headcount, in fact, I have more responsibility,this whole thing can fall ten times harder so I should be more concerned about every single penny

key_insights.insight: Financial control issues can lead to business making decisions blindly.
key_insights.quote: In the early days, we had pretty poor financial control. We were making decisions totally blind

key_insights.insight: Forgiveness of those who have wronged you is essential for peace and rising above them.
key_insights.quote: forgiveness is letting a prisoner go. And as they run off into the distance, realising that you were the prisoner the whole time

actionable_advice.advice: Question your profit and loss statements and ask for it to be done in a way that makes sense to you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Challenge your accountant and ask them to explain the data in a format you can readily understand. Don't be afraid to ask for it to be done differently.

actionable_advice.advice: Don't be afraid to cancel contacts if you feel they are not worth their nominal fee.
actionable_advice.how_to: Evaluate the revenues you generate compared to the consultant's fees, and think about if the contract adds responsibility.

actionable_advice.advice: Be more guarded about the information you share with the team.
actionable_advice.how_to: Don't discuss what is discussed with the accountants with the senior team, rather give them enough to push on and do what they need to do for the business but not the granular detail

actionable_advice.advice: Implement real-time financial tracking in your business.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use a service that integrates directly with your bank feed and provides up-to-date profit and loss data within a week of month-end.

memorable_stories: Mark Stringer recounts paying all employee wages and then having no money left for lunch.
Stringer describes discovering messages from his senior team plotting to start a competing agency, finding evidence of IP theft and solicitation of staff.
Stringer shares his experience of being caught in the London Bridge terrorist attack and the lasting impact it had on his perspective.
Stringer describes how nacho's saved his life during the london terrorist attack
contrarian_claims: It's good to spend a lot of time with those you love even if that means your wife works in the business. (Most people find it hard working so closely with partners).
It is bad business sense to get addicted to pitching (however, is something agency owners have to do)
Although hard, he tries not to hold what the senior team did against them and thinks the lad culture of bravado overtook them.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - E6 Mark's Diary - Betrayal, backstabbing and death
